response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack engineer with strong web, mobile, and some desktop/embedded-adjacent experience, but the profile does not match this embedded networking security role. The position has hard requirements the resume does not satisfy: native-level Japanese (the candidate lists no Japanese), strong C for UNIX development (only limited C/C++ at National Instruments years ago), Linux kernel investigation and source modification, and networking-security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3, switch firmware) — none of which appear. The bulk of the experience is TypeScript/Node/DLang web services, which is largely orthogonal to the role.
strengths: Deep, long-tenured software engineering experience with strong craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, testing focus)
Some low-level/systems exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments, C++ audio thesis on Linux, robotics work
Comfortable across many languages and Linux/Docker environments, suggesting adaptability
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, which is a stated native-level hard requirement
No demonstrated Linux kernel modification or embedded networking security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3)
C for UNIX experience is minimal and dated; primary expertise is web/full-stack, not embedded firmware

key_factors.factor: Limited C/UNIX and no Linux kernel work
